records.ety: [AS. naes, ns; akin to Icel. nes, Sw. näs, Dan. naes, and E. nose. √ 261. See Nose.]

defs: A promontory; a cape; a headland.
notes: ☞ Ness is frequently used as a suffix in the names of places and promontories; as, Sheerness.

records.ety: [AS. -ness, -nyss, -nys; akin to OS. -nissi, nussi, D. -nis, OHG. -nissa, -nassī, -nussī, G. -nis, -niss, Goth. -inasus.]

defs: A suffix used to form abstract nouns expressive of quality or state; as, goodness, greatness.
